recentNews.headline: OpenAI-Broadcom custom chip deal hits $18 billion financing snag
recentNews.whyItMatters: Broadcom reportedly will fund the first production phase only if Microsoft commits to buy ~40% of the chips, exposing customer-credit and offtake risk that could delay revenue recognition on one of AVGO's marquee AI backlog wins.
recentNews.dateNote: Exact day approximate; article timestamp not exposed in search. Headline, publication, and substance verified.

recentNews.headline: Report says Google may expand custom AI chip work with Marvell, AVGO shares slip
recentNews.whyItMatters: Signals Broadcom's most lucrative TPU franchise is not single-sourced, introducing competitive share-loss risk that pressures the long-term ASIC revenue trajectory underpinning the stock's premium multiple.

recentNews.headline: Meta commits to 1 gigawatt of custom chips on Broadcom tech as Hock Tan exits Meta board
recentNews.whyItMatters: Locks in a multi-gigawatt MTIA (2nm) customer that diversifies AVGO's AI accelerator base, while CEO Hock Tan's move off Meta's board to an advisory role removes a governance conflict-of-interest overhang. Note: this is Meta's board, not Broadcom's; Tan remains Broadcom CEO.

recentNews.headline: Broadcom expands custom chip deals with Google and Anthropic
recentNews.whyItMatters: Anthropic gains access to ~3.5 gigawatts of Google TPU capacity through 2029, deepening multi-year backlog visibility that supports the bull case for AVGO's AI compute pipeline.

recentNews.headline: Broadcom beats Q1 FY26 estimates as AI revenue doubles, guides Q2 to about $22 billion
recentNews.whyItMatters: Record $19.3B revenue (up 29%) with AI revenue of $8.4B up 106% and a 47% Q2 growth guide reinforces the AI accelerator/networking growth thesis and the path to $100B+ AI chip revenue by 2027 that drives the valuation.

recentNews.headline: Broadcom reveals mystery $10 billion customer is Anthropic, discloses $11 billion follow-on order
recentNews.whyItMatters: Confirms Anthropic as a fourth major custom-silicon anchor with a combined ~$21B in orders for late-2026 delivery, materially de-risking forward AI backlog and validating the multi-customer ASIC platform that differentiates AVGO from merchant GPU vendors.

otherFlaggedItems.headline: Broadcom faces mounting EU scrutiny over VMware licensing curbs
otherFlaggedItems.note: Regulatory item not in top 6 but worth monitoring.
